summary: Warm audience recording captures a soaring show highlighted by Shakedown‑Bertha, Terrapin Station and an incendiary Playing in the Band.
review: The audience tape delivers a spacious, warm ambience that puts listeners right inside the famed "Barn" – despite a few harsh edits between song cuts. Musically the concert is praised for its blazing second set, with Shakedown Street into Bertha, Terrapin Station, Saint of Circumstance and a scorching Playing in the Band standing out, while vocals from Jerry, Bob and Brent receive special kudos.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, highlighting excellent performance and a good‑quality audience recording; only minor technical complaints appear.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm, spacious sound with clear crowd ambience; some listeners note harsh cuts between Shakedown→Bertha and Playin'→Good Lovin', but overall the tape is listenable and immersive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is described as an outstanding, scorching stretch that mixed things up and kept the audience on fire; the first set flows smoothly with a memorable opening Jack Straw.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals and guitar praised for energy; Garcia’s Cornell alma‑mater tease and vocal work highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass bombs during Shakedown Street noted as tasty; Phil fans love this version.
band_member_comments.Bob: Strong vocal performance mentioned alongside Jerry and Brent.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s vocals (and keyboard presence) called incredible; early Brent‑era vibe appreciated.
